2606.24783.title: Paying to Know: Micro-Transaction Markets for Verified Product Information in Agentic E-Commerce
2606.24783.paper_summary: Commercial NLP treats the shopping chatbot as a recommender or a conversion tool: its job is to match a user to a catalogue entry and close a sale. We argue that the arrival of agent-native micro-payment rails (e.g., x402, AP2) changes what is scarce. When the buyer is an autonomous agent that can investigate exhaustively, the bottleneck is no longer matching products but acquiring trustworthy, decision-relevant information about them. We envision agentic e-commerce as a micro-transaction market for verified information: buyer agents spend fractions of a cent to progressively unlock seller- and reviewer-supplied data -- service histories, third-party test reports, bills of materials, audited sales and support metrics -- paid for a la carte under a freemium model, with reviewer trust scored reputationally. We sketch the architecture of such a market and argue that it rewards genuine product quality and yields truer competition than ranking-based storefronts. We then translate the vision into concrete NLP problems -- cost-optimal information acquisition, data pricing and negotiation, real-time entity resolution, grounded value exchange, and privacy-preserving persona modelling -- and argue that these, not chat fluency, deserve the field's attention.
2606.24783.paper_summary_zh: 商業自然語言處理將購物聊天機器人視為推薦或轉換工具：它的工作是將用戶與目錄條目匹配並完成銷售。我們認為，自主代理微支付系統（例如，x402，AP2）的出現改變了稀缺的定義。當買家是一個可以徹底調查的自主代理時，瓶頸不再是匹配產品，而是獲取有關產品的可靠、決策相關的信息。我們想像中的代理電子商務是一個為驗證信息提供微交易市場：買方代理花費幾分之一美分逐步解鎖賣方和評論者提供的數據——服務歷史、第三方測試報告、物料清單、經過審計的銷售和支持指標——根據自選模式按需付費，評論者的信任則以聲譽評分。我們勾勒了這樣一個市場的架構，並認為它獎勵真正的產品質量，並比基於排名的商店產生更真實的競爭。然後，我們將這一願景轉化為具體的自然語言處理問題——成本最優的信息獲取、數據定價和談判、實時實體解析、基於價值的交換以及保護隱私的角色建模——並認為這些問題，而不是聊天流利度，才值得這一領域的關注。
2606.24783.author: Filippos Ventirozos et.al.
